metadata.title: Late Social Security Payment? What to Check First
metadata.description: If your Social Security payment is late or missing, check the scheduled date and learn the official next steps from SSA.

hero.title: Late Social Security Payment?
hero.description: First confirm the scheduled date. If an electronic payment does not arrive on the scheduled pay date, SSA says to contact your bank or financial institution first.
hero.tip: Independent tool. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Social Security Administration.

checker.title: Confirm the scheduled payment date
checker.description: Use the checker to estimate the scheduled date before deciding whether the payment is late.

steps.title: What to check before reporting a missing payment
steps.description: This site cannot report a payment issue for you. Use official channels for anything involving your benefit record.

items.title: Check the scheduled date
items.description: Make sure the date has passed and remember that weekends and federal holidays can move scheduled dates earlier.

items.title: Contact your bank first
items.description: SSA says to contact your bank or financial institution first for electronic payments that do not arrive on the scheduled date.

items.title: Contact Social Security
items.description: If you still need to report a late, missing, or stolen payment, contact Social Security directly through official channels.

official_sources.title: Official sources for late payments
official_sources.description: Use the checker for quick planning, then use SSA and Treasury pages for official calendar publications, benefit records, and payment-method changes.
official_sources.tip: Social Security Dates is independent and does not collect sensitive personal or banking information.

items.title: SSA payment calendar
items.description: Official SSA page for Social Security and SSI payment schedules and calendar publications.

items.title: SSA 2026 schedule PDF
items.description: SSA Publication No. 05-10031 for 2026 scheduled benefit payment dates.

items.title: SSA missing payment FAQ
items.description: Official guidance for late, missing, or stolen Social Security payments.

items.title: Go Direct
items.description: U.S. Treasury resource for electronic federal benefit payment information.

items.question: Can this site report a missing payment?
items.answer: No. This site only estimates scheduled dates. It cannot file a case, access your record, or contact SSA for you.

items.question: What phone number does SSA list for missing payments?
items.answer: SSA lists 1-800-772-1213 and TTY 1-800-325-0778 for late, missing, or stolen Social Security payments after checking with your bank.

items.question: Should I enter bank information here?
items.answer: No. This checker never asks for bank account, routing number, Direct Express details, SSN, or login information.

cta.title: Start with the scheduled date
cta.description: Then use official SSA and bank channels for anything involving your account or payment status.
